[Sun 2026-04-05 08:57 UTC] [Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]: # ULTRAPLAN: Complete Visual Redesign — AlphaClaw Apex Style

You have up to 20 minutes to think deeply. Do NOT execute anything. Produce a comprehensive, actionable plan.

## Task
Redesign ALL web applications on this server to match AlphaClaw Apex's UI/UX design language. This includes page transition animations, component styling, typography, and interaction patterns. The goal is to make every app feel like a unified, premium product.

## AlphaClaw Apex Design Language (from video analysis)

### Visual DNA
1. **Monospace-first typography** — JetBrains Mono for ALL text (headings, body, labels, code). Not just code blocks.
2. **Cyan accent color (#63ebff / #00efff)** — primary interactive color. NOT indigo.
3. **Background: #0d121b** with subtle grid texture overlay (48px grid lines at ~2.4% white opacity) — NOT gradient orbs
4. **Sidebar background: #0f141f**, content area: #0f1521
5. **Border color: rgba(255,255,255,0.06)** default, stronger on hover
6. **Text hierarchy: primary #c9d1d9, muted #6e7681, dim #424854**

### Page Transition Animation (CRITICAL — this is the hero feature)
Between every page/section switch:
1. Current content fades out (200ms)
2. Full-screen dark overlay appears
3. Large centered icon (section emoji) fades in + slight scale-up from 0.8→1.0
4. Section name text fades in below icon (0.5s after icon)
5. Hold for ~1 second
6. Icon + text fade out
7. New page content fades in from bottom (translateY(20px) → 0) with stagger on child elements

Implementation: This should be a shared JS module (transitions.js) that wraps all navigation. When clicking a nav item:
```
navTo('/chat/', '💬', 'Council Chat')
```

### Component Patterns
- **Cards:** Very minimal — just `border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)`, background slightly elevated, `border-radius: 10px`. No glass-morphism blur.
- **Buttons:** Cyan gradient button (primary), secondary = subtle border + bg, danger = red tint. All have 1px border.
- **Sidebar:** Left sidebar with grouped sections. Active item has **cyan left border (3px)** + slightly brighter background. Hover state is subtle bg change.
- **Stat cards:** Large monospace numbers, small uppercase labels below. Minimal decoration.
- **Toggle switches:** Cyan when on, pill-shaped, smooth transition.
- **Tables:** Minimal, no zebra stripes, subtle row border-bottom only.
- **Inputs:** Dark background (rgba(0,0,0,0.3)), border on focus brightens.
- **Badges/Pills:** Small, rounded, colored background matching status.

### Micro-animations
- Cards fade-in with stagger (each card delayed 50ms after previous)
- Numbers have count-up animation on initial load
- Sidebar items have smooth 150ms hover transition
- Loading states use a subtle pulse animation (not spinner)
- Toast notifications slide up from bottom-right

### Embedded Terminal
- Monospace, dark background (#0a0e14)
- Green text for success, red for errors, cyan for info
- Scrollable with thin scrollbar

### Color Tokens (from AlphaClaw theme.css)
```css
--bg: #0d121b;
--bg-sidebar: #0f141f;
--bg-content: #0f1521;
--bg-hover: rgba(99, 235, 255, 0.05);
--bg-active: rgba(99, 235, 255, 0.08);
--border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06);
--border-strong: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.11);
--text: #c9d1d9;
--text-muted: #6e7681;
--text-dim: #424854;
--text-bright: #f3f4f6;
--accent: #63ebff;
--accent-dim: rgba(99, 235, 255, 0.4);
--orange: #d98a58;
--keyword: #ff7b72;
--string: #a5d6ff;
--number: #79c0ff;
--status-error: #fca5a5;
--status-warning: #fde047;
--status-success: #86efac;
--status-info: #a5f3fc;
```

## ALL Apps to Redesign

### Tier 1: MAGI v2 (18 pages) — ~/magi-v2/frontend/
Already migrated to shared infrastructure. Now need visual redesign.
Pages: dashboard, login, chat, roundtable, vault, knowledge, mission, monitor, analytics, tasks, files, leads, icat, pipeline, broadcast, notifications, reports, settings

### Tier 2: Council Room Legacy (active pages) — ~/council-room/frontend/
- it-support.html (808 lines) — emergency controls, agent models, Uriel chat, quick commands
- voice-call/index.html — WebSocket voice call with agents
- admin.html — admin dashboard

### Tier 3: Paul Simulator (D&D App) — ~/dnd-app/frontend/
- index.html (1815 lines) — main D&D companion (character sheets, dice, initiative, spells)
- guide.html (1009 lines) — D&D guide
- flow.html (322 lines) — flow view

## Your Output Must Include

### 1. Shared Design System Update
Exact CSS to replace the current ~/magi-v2/frontend/shared/core.css with AlphaClaw Apex tokens:
- New color scheme (cyan accent, grid texture, monospace typography)
- New background treatment (grid texture, NOT orbs)
- Updated component styles
- Transition animation system

### 2. Page Transition Module Spec
`/shared/transitions.js` — detailed spec for the page transition animation system:
- How it intercepts navigation
- The animation sequence (timing, easing, elements)
- Icon/label config per page
- How it integrates with existing nav.js
- Fallback for direct URL access (no transition, just fade-in)

### 3. Navigation Update
Update nav.js spec to match Apex sidebar:
- Grouped sections with small uppercase headers
- Active item = cyan left border + brighter bg
- Monospace font
- Collapse/expand behavior

### 4. Component Migration Guide
For each component type, show exact CSS change from current → Apex style:
- Cards (glass → minimal border)
- Buttons (indigo → cyan)
- Inputs/selects
- Badges/tags
- Modals
- Toast
- Tables
- Stat cards with count-up

### 5. Staggered Entry Animation Spec
How cards/items animate in on page load:
- CSS @keyframes
- JS to add stagger classes
- Timing (50ms per item)

### 6. Page-by-Page Visual Changes
For EACH of the ~25 active pages across all 3 tiers:
- Current look → Apex look (what changes)
- Specific color/font/layout changes
- Animation additions
- Priority (P0/P1/P2)

### 7. Paul Simulator (D&D) Specific Plan
The D&D app has its own aesthetic (fantasy/medieval). Plan for:
- Keep D&D flavor but adopt Apex's technical polish
- Dark theme aligned with Apex
- Monospace for game stats, but serif/display for flavor text?
- Dice roll animations
- Character sheet layout improvements

### 8. Execution Plan (4 Agents)
Split into 4 parallel work streams:
- Agent 1: Shared infrastructure update (core.css, components.css, transitions.js, nav.js update)
- Agent 2: MAGI core pages visual update
- Agent 3: MAGI ops + business pages visual update
- Agent 4: Council Room legacy + DnD app visual update

### 9. Count-Up Animation for Stats
Spec for animated number counting:
- Start from 0, count up to target
- Duration proportional to number size
- Use requestAnimationFrame
- Easing function (ease-out)
- Trigger on scroll-into-view (IntersectionObserver)

### 10. Quality Checklist
- [ ] Every page uses JetBrains Mono
- [ ] Every page has grid texture background (not orbs)
- [ ] Cyan (#63ebff) is the accent everywhere
- [ ] Page transitions work between all nav items
- [ ] Cards have staggered entrance animation
- [ ] Stats have count-up animation
- [ ] Sidebar has cyan left-border active state
- [ ] All buttons use cyan gradient
- [ ] All inputs have dark bg + border-focus
- [ ] Mobile responsive maintained
- [ ] No visual regression on functionality

## Rules
- DO NOT execute any code
- You MAY use read to examine current files
- Focus on THINKING and SPECIFYING
- Be extremely precise about CSS values, timing, and animation sequences
- The plan must be detailed enough for 4 agents to execute without ambiguity
- Reference the AlphaClaw theme.css tokens throughout
I'll start by examining the current shared infrastructure files and key pages across all three tiers to understand what needs to change. Let me read the critical files first.
